reviews.review_description:
So, I recently played this horror game called Bone Monkey, and honestly, it freaked me out way more than I expected. It’s not one of those loud, jump-scare-heavy games—it's the kind of game that messes with your head and makes you feel super uneasy the whole time.

The story is about being stuck in this creepy abandon arcade with this weird monster called Yourup. But it’s not just some random scary thing chasing you. As I played, I realized the Bone Monkey was kind of tied to the character’s own fears and guilt. It made the whole thing feel way more personal and creepy—like I wasn’t just running from a monster, but from something deeper.

What really got me was the atmosphere. The mansion looks so dark and eerie, with creepy dolls and strange symbols everywhere. And the sounds? Oh man, every little creak or whisper had me jumping, even when I told myself it was nothing. The game does a great job of building this slow, creeping fear that just gets under your skin.

Playing felt more like sneaking around than fighting. I had to be careful—hiding in shadows, staying quiet, and hoping the Bone Monkey wouldn’t find me. The thing is, the Bone Monkey doesn’t follow a set path, so I never really felt safe. It kept me on edge the whole time, and that kind of unpredictable fear was way worse than any jump scare.

What I liked most is that the game isn’t just about scaring you. It made me think about how fear works—how sometimes the scariest monsters are the ones inside your own head. That made the story stick with me even after I stopped playing.
My friend jellywrom made this game and it is the best game that he has made. It’s genuinely fun and I just like the idea of different game modes and I’m excited for the rest.￼
I really enjoy playing this game I recommend playing it if you are bored

reviews.review_title: I haven’t even played and it’s good

reviews.review_description: This is pretty much your average monketag fangame with the basics. Also add multiplayer
